examples.title: Algorithm: Transaction Structure
examples.description: This pseudocode demonstrates the structure of a transaction in a blockchain.
examples.code:
ALGORITHM TransactionStructure
INPUT: inputs (list of transaction inputs)
OUTPUT: transaction (structured data)

BEGIN
 transaction.version = 1
 transaction.inputs = []
 FOR EACH input IN inputs
 transaction.inputs.append(input)
 END FOR
 transaction.outputs = []
 FOR EACH output IN outputs
 transaction.outputs.append(output)
 END FOR
 transaction.locktime = 0
 RETURN transaction
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode defines the basic structure of a transaction, including version, inputs, outputs, and locktime.

examples.title: Python: Transaction Class
examples.description: This Python implementation demonstrates a Transaction class with methods for adding inputs and outputs.
examples.explanation: This Python class represents a transaction with methods for adding inputs and outputs, calculating the fee, and printing the transaction details.
